message:
feat(ipc): tick 200 handle_client_with_context_with_cx request-line read cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

handle_client_with_context_with_cx (ipc.rs L1326) takes the caller's
cx by value and spawns from run_with_context_with_cx's accept loop
with a child cx clone. Inside the handler body the request-line
read was using ambient `compat_unix::next_line(&mut lines)` — the
cx-first sibling `next_line_with_cx(&cx, &mut lines)` was available
(runtime_compat.rs L1389) but unused.

The ambient variant returns whenever the stream yields but doesn't
observe cx cancellation at any entry checkpoint. A client that
connects and stalls without sending a full line would block the
per-connection handler indefinitely, even when the outer IPC server
cx has been cancelled.

The cx-first variant inserts a pre-read cx.checkpoint() so a
cancel fired mid-handler (e.g. during server shutdown) lands at
the read boundary rather than after the client eventually writes
or drops.

Tick 200 switches L1338 to `next_line_with_cx(&cx, &mut lines)`.
Matches the pattern already used by native_events::handle_connection_with_cx
(native_events.rs L465).

Legacy `handle_client_with_context` (non-cx path) and the client-side
send_request_with_id_with_cx (which already uses next_line) are
unchanged.

Verification: 98 / 98 ipc:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including
shutdown_does_not_hang_with_idle_client_connection — the specific
test that exercises an idle-client-stall shutdown race.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
